seoTitle: How to Embed Loom Videos in GoHighLevel Websites
metaDescription: Learn how to embed Loom videos into your GoHighLevel website to boost engagement with tutorials, demos, and testimonials.
intro: Adding Loom videos to your GoHighLevel website turns a static page into something more dynamic, whether you're sharing a product demo, tutorial, or customer testimonial. This guide covers finding your embed code, inserting it into your site, and optimizing how the video displays. It's aimed at entrepreneurs, marketers, and beginners who want their website to feel more engaging without needing advanced technical skills.

steps.heading: Locate your Loom embed code
steps.body: Open your Loom account and find the embed code for the video you want to share, which you'll need before adding it to your site.

steps.heading: Insert the video into your GoHighLevel website
steps.body: Add the embed code to the relevant section of your website builder so the Loom video displays where you want it.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for displaying social proof and demos
steps.body: Position embedded videos where they naturally support your message, such as near a product description or testimonial section.

steps.heading: Customize your embedded video
steps.body: Adjust display settings where available so the video fits well within your site's overall design and doesn't feel out of place.

steps.heading: Use video to increase engagement
steps.body: Videos like product demos or client transformations can help drive higher visitor engagement and improve conversion rates on your site.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Loom account to embed videos in GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: Loom's embedding capability generally works with the embed code your account provides, so check your specific plan's video sharing options.

faq.question: Can I embed multiple Loom videos on one page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add more than one embed code to a page as long as it fits your website's layout and purpose.

faq.question: What kind of content works best as an embedded video?
faq.answer: Tutorials, product demos, and customer testimonials are commonly used since they add clear, visual value to a page.

faq.question: Will an embedded video slow down my website?
faq.answer: Embedded videos are generally hosted externally by Loom, which helps minimize the impact on your website's own loading performance.

faq.question: Is this feature accessible for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, the embedding process is designed to be simple enough for beginners while still useful for more experienced website builders.
